name: Qwen3.7-Max
archDisplay: MoE, exact expert configuration undisclosed; positioned as an agent-first reasoning model
note: Agent-first flagship with 1M-token context (up from 256K on the prior generation); Alibaba reports 1,000+ consecutive tool calls and a 35-hour autonomous kernel-optimization run in internal testing (not independently verified). Not open-weight; no HF checkpoints as of this research.

results.notes: Listed as 'SWE-Verified' (SWE-bench Verified) on Alibaba Cloud's own official launch blog, confirmed via direct fetch; independently corroborated at the identical value by three separate secondary write-ups (DataCamp, a Weights & Biases report, and CodingFleet), all of which explicitly cite the Alibaba blog as their source. This is the same figure the corpus's existing verificationNotes flagged as 'could not independently confirm' -- now confirmed directly.
Confirmed via direct fetch of the official Alibaba Cloud launch blog; cross-corroborated by DataCamp, W&B, and CodingFleet write-ups citing the same blog.
Humanity's Last Exam, confirmed via direct fetch of the official Alibaba Cloud launch blog.
Confirmed via direct fetch of the official Alibaba Cloud launch blog's 'General Capabilities & Multilingualism' section.
Confirmed via direct fetch of the official Alibaba Cloud launch blog.

From the 'General Capability' benchmark table embedded as an image in Alibaba Cloud's official Qwen3.7 launch blog (the table is a PNG graphic, not present in the page's HTML/text, so a naive text scrape misses it -- confirmed via direct visual inspection of the embedded table image). Qwen3.7-Max scored 89.6 vs Opus-4.6 Max 89.7, K2.6 Thinking 87.1, GLM-5.1 Thinking 86.3, DS-V4-Pro Max 87.5, Qwen3.6-Plus 88.5.
Same embedded table image, 'General Capability' section. Qwen3.7-Max scored 95.0 vs Opus-4.6 Max 95.2, K2.6 Thinking 95.3, GLM-5.1 Thinking 94.3, DS-V4-Pro Max 94.8, Qwen3.6-Plus 94.5.

Distinct row from IFBench (already tracked at 79.1) in the same table image -- IFEval and IFBench are separate taxonomy slugs and separate rows in Alibaba's own table. Qwen3.7-Max scored 94.3, tied with Qwen3.6-Plus and just behind K2.6 Thinking/GLM-5.1 Thinking (94.5 each).
From the 'Multilingualism' section of the same table image (Multilingual MMLU). Qwen3.7-Max scored 90.3, second only to Opus-4.6 Max's 90.6 among the six compared models.

GDPval-AA v2 ('agentic real-world work tasks', normalized as (Elo-500)/2000), independently measured by Artificial Analysis on their live Qwen3.7-Max model page -- not vendor self-reported. Qwen3.7-Max scored 39%, behind Claude Opus 4.8 (max) at 55% and DeepSeek V4 Pro (max) at 40%, but ahead of Kimi K2.6 (35%) and Nemotron 3 Ultra (33%). This is one of the 9 components of AA's own Intelligence Index (v4.1) for this model. [SCALE FIX 2026-07-17: corrected from a normalized percentage (39%) to this taxonomy's actual raw 0-2000-point Elo scale (matching the majority convention used by e.g. Gemini 3.1 Pro=1317, Claude Fable 5=1932). Converted via Artificial Analysis's own documented formula, normalized=(Elo-500)/2000, i.e. raw=39*20+500=1280. This formula is independently cross-validated against Kimi K2.7 Code's directly-disclosed embedded-JSON raw Elo (1186.79 for a displayed 34%, matching the formula almost exactly).]

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 11.8% / Score 31.1% (rank 41, listed as 'qwen3-7-max'). Score component used per corpus convention.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 15/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Qwen3.7 Max" (Alibaba). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via multiple independent secondary sources (MarkTechPost, VentureBeat-cited coverage, Yotta Labs, digitalapplied, Alibaba Cloud's own community blog 'Qwen3.7: The Agent Frontier') plus a working OpenRouter listing (qwen/qwen3.7-max) and official Alibaba Cloud Model Studio pricing-page entry -- this is a real, currently-available model, not a rumor. Announcement date has minor variance across sources (May 19-21, 2026); used May 20 (Alibaba Cloud Summit date, matching the majority of sources) as releaseDate. Context length also varies by source: most (OpenRouter, Yotta Labs, digitalapplied) state 1M tokens; one direct fetch of the Alibaba Cloud blog surfaced a '256K' figure that appears to be a benchmark-footnote qualifier rather than the max context -- flagged as uncertain. Pricing is the list-price tier ($2.50/$7.50 per M, Intl) confirmed via Alibaba's own pricing page; a time-limited 50% launch promo ($1.25/$3.75) is also in effect per OpenRouter/Yotta Labs but list price was used per convention. Architecture (param count, MoE expert split) is undisclosed across all sources -- flagged null per corpus convention for undisclosed flagship models (matches existing Qwen3-Max entry's treatment). Benchmark scores (GPQA 92.4, SWE-bench Pro 60.6, Terminal-Bench 2.0-Terminus 69.7) were corroborated by two independent direct fetches (Alibaba Cloud's own blog + Yotta Labs); other cited figures (SWE-bench Verified 80.4, HMMT, IMOAnswerBench, Apex, MRCR-v2, MCP-Mark) could not be independently confirmed via a direct-render fetch of a primary source and were omitted rather than guessed.

2026-07-16: Checked all 5 flagged-missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu) against official launch materials, independent leaderboards, and sibling-model cross-reference; all 5 resolve to real absences, not oversights. (1) Official Alibaba Cloud launch blog (text scrape + accessibility-tree read of the embedded benchmark-table image) contains no plain-MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval rows -- the family has replaced these saturated legacy benchmarks with harder successors (MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux/MMLU-ProX for knowledge, HMMT 2026 Feb/IMOAnswerBench/Apex for math, LiveCodeBench/SciCode/SWE-bench-Pro for coding). (2) Independent confirmation: Artificial Analysis's live Qwen3.7-Max model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/qwen3-7-max) likewise shows no MMLU/MATH-500/AIME/HumanEval scores, and explicitly states the model "does not support image input. It can only process text" -- settling MMMU as structurally inapplicable, not merely unpublished. llm-stats.com's dedicated model page also carries no scores for any of the four remaining benchmarks despite tracking them site-wide. Vals.ai and Epoch AI pages for this model exist (Vals Index 57.49%, Epoch ECI 153/rank 22) but their per-benchmark breakdowns are JS-rendered and did not expose MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval sub-scores through static fetch; no further evidence found there. (3) Sibling-model cross-reference: Qwen3.7-Plus (same generation, vision-enabled) reports mmlu-pro/mmlu-redux/mmmlu/mmmu-pro/mathvision but never plain mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval either -- confirming this is a generation-wide house style, not a Max-specific gap. Qwen3.6-Plus (prior generation) did report aime (95.3) and mmmu (86), showing AIME and plain MMMU were both retired between the 3.6 and 3.7 generations in favor of HMMT and MMMU-Pro/vision-benchmark-suite respectively. Two low-quality aggregator sites (pricepertoken.com claiming MMLU 93.7%; automatio.ai claiming MMLU 92.8%/MATH 94.8%/AIME 99.7%/HumanEval 94.5%) were checked and rejected as sources: automatio.ai's own page contradicts already-verified primary-source figures for this same model (e.g. LiveCodeBench 78.2% vs. the confirmed official 91.6%, HLE 38.2% vs. confirmed 41.4%), and a third aggregator (benchlm.ai) mislabels the corpus's existing mmmlu=90.3 entry as "MMMU," demonstrating this class of site conflates/fabricates benchmark identities and cannot be trusted absent primary corroboration. All 5 gaps therefore recorded as omitted_no_evidence.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep pass on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, new source types only): Systematically checked all 6 prescribed new source types not covered by the prior pass (which had already exhausted official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena overall leaderboard, and BenchLM). None produced a credible, citable score for any of the four gaps.

(1) Stanford HELM: Fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest/ and crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest/ directly -- both are JS-rendered SPAs that return only a page header via static fetch, no leaderboard data. Supplementary web search of HELM's own GitHub/docs (crfm-helm.readthedocs.io, stanford-crfm/helm issues) turned up references to Qwen3.5/Qwen3-Next model integrations (evaluated via the Together AI platform) but zero mentions of "Qwen3.7-Max" anywhere in HELM's model roster or output. Conclusion: not present in HELM as of this check, most likely because HELM's model set had not yet been updated to include this specific proprietary, API-only model released 2026-05-20.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: Ran five distinct targeted searches ("Qwen3.7-Max MMLU site:arxiv.org", "Qwen3.7-Max MATH benchmark arxiv baseline", comparison-table searches, and a direct semanticscholar.org query). All returned only papers about earlier/different Qwen3 variants (Qwen3 Technical Report, Qwen3-VL Technical Report, Qwen3-32B/235B, Qwen2.5-Math) -- none used "Qwen3.7-Max" as a baseline or comparison point. No paper found.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: Searched for lm-evaluation-harness results referencing "qwen3.7-max"; found only unrelated EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness issues about older Qwen3 (non-.7) IFEval/MMLU quirks. No committed results.json or benchmark-repo run log exists for this model, consistent with it being a closed, API-only model (no local-harness route, and no committed API-harness run found either).

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's model page is pricing/context-only, no benchmark scores. Scale AI SEAL: only found a 2025-era tweet about the (non-.7) "Qwen3" family going live on SEAL, nothing specific to Qwen3.7-Max. LiveBench.ai: Qwen3.7-Max does not appear on the main LiveBench table (its LiveCodeBench-v6 score, already in the corpus, is the only overlap). PapersWithCode: no page/leaderboard entries found. LMArena's category-specific pages (checked as the arena-leaderboard/category angle beyond the prior pass's overall-Elo check) show Qwen3.7-Max-Preview ranked #7 in the Math category and #3 in Coding by Arena Elo -- but these are relative Elo rankings from pairwise human voting, not accuracy scores on the MATH/AIME/HumanEval benchmarks, so they are not a substitutable data point for the missing slugs and were not added. Together AI's model page (together.ai/models/qwen37-max) is the one page that superficially appeared to report "AIME 2025: 91.6" and "MATH500: 80.4" -- but these values are exact duplicates of this model's already-confirmed LiveCodeBench (91.6) and SWE-bench Verified (80.4) scores respectively, a strong signature of a table-column scraping/mirroring error rather than genuine distinct measurements; rejected as unreliable, same failure class as the previously-rejected automatio.ai and pricepertoken.com.

(5) Non-English sources: Searched Chinese-language coverage specifically. A Zhihu article by 302.AI's "benchmark lab" (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2042657228990174422) was fetched directly and confirmed to report only self-reported Alibaba figures already in the corpus (HLE 41.4, GPQA/HMMT referenced without numbers) plus 302.AI's own qualitative/non-benchmark testing (logic puzzles, SVG/Three.js generation) -- no independent MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers. Two other Chinese sites surfaced by search (aitoollab.cn, qbitai.com) could not be reached (DNS resolution failures for both domains from this environment) and so could not be verified; a search-engine-synthesized claim of "MMLU 89.7%" attributed to one of these unreachable pages was not used, both because it could not be directly verified against the live page and because 89.7 is suspiciously close to this model's already-confirmed MMLU-Pro score of 89.6, suggesting the same MMLU/MMLU-Pro conflation error already documented for other rejected aggregators in this file's prior verification notes.

(6) Sibling models: No new sibling comparison tables found beyond what the prior pass already documented (Qwen3.7-Plus and Qwen3.6-Plus checked previously; neither reports plain mmlu/math/aime/humaneval for the 3.7 generation).

Net result: all four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) remain genuine absences after checking all 6 new source types -- not oversights, and not resolvable by any source type available as of 2026-07-17. No additions made; nothing in the existing 14-entry results array was changed.

2026-07-18 (round-3 final pass on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, prescribed NEW source types only -- Wayback Machine, conference/keynote transcripts, investor/press materials, GitHub SDK-repo issues/discussions, and not-yet-checked third-party analysis blogs): All 5 prescribed source types were genuinely attempted; none produced a credible, citable score for any of the four gaps.

(1) Wayback Machine: This source type was ATTEMPTED BUT TECHNICALLY INACCESSIBLE in this research environment, not merely checked-and-clean -- flagging this honestly rather than claiming a clean negative. Confirmed via the archive.org "available" API that snapshots do exist (Alibaba launch blog: closest snapshot 2026-06-04T15:34:13Z at http://web.archive.org/web/20260604153413/https://www.alibabacloud.com/blog/qwen3-7-the-agent-frontier_603154; Artificial Analysis Qwen3.7-Max page: closest snapshot 2026-05-25T22:45:30Z). However, every method tried to actually fetch the archived page content was blocked: the WebFetch tool refuses the web.archive.org host outright ("Claude Code is unable to fetch from web.archive.org"), including via the raw id_ passthrough mode; a direct curl from the sandbox got HTTP 429 (rate-limited) on the first attempt and then "connection refused" on repeated retries with backoff (5s/8s/15s/20s waits); proxy workarounds (r.jina.ai, corsproxy.io, 12ft.io, timetravel.mementoweb.org) each failed for independent reasons (401 needing an API key, 403, TLS error, and DNS/connection failure respectively). Net effect: could not compare archived-vs-live benchmark tables for either the launch blog or the AA tracker page. This is a genuine environment/access limitation, recorded transparently rather than folded into a false "checked, nothing found."

(2) Conference talks / keynote transcripts: Located the actual official launch video ("Meet Qwen3.7 Max -- our latest flagship, made for the Agent Era," Alibaba Cloud's YouTube channel, watch?v=xDdpoqp5HhI, published ~2026-05-21) and the "Qwen Cloud on Qwen Conference Keynote" video (Qwen Cloud's channel, watch?v=Y3uJniSBZ6I, ~2026-06-01, covering the inaugural Qwen Conference 2026 in Singapore). Confirmed both exist via YouTube's oembed metadata endpoint (title/author), but full description text and captions/transcripts were not retrievable -- direct WebFetch of the watch pages redirected to Google's bot-check ("/sorry/") page, and a direct curl with a browser user-agent also hit a 302 bot-check redirect. Fell back to targeted web search of secondary coverage of these events (TechNode, Computer Weekly, Omdia, MarkTechPost, officechai) -- none of the recap articles quote a keynote slide with plain MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers; all cite the same self-reported figures already in the corpus (GPQA, SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench, HMMT, Apex, MCP-Atlas).

(3) Investor / press materials: Used SEC EDGAR's full-text search API directly (efts.sec.gov) restricted to Alibaba Group Holding (CIK 1577552) and found exactly one 6-K filing exhibit mentioning "Qwen3.7" verbatim: EX-99.1 of the 6-K filed 2026-06-18 (accession 0001193125-26-274928, https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1577552/000119312526274928/d133513dex991.pdf -- Alibaba's FY2026 20-F annual report). Downloaded and full-text-searched the entire 270-page PDF: the only Qwen3.7-Max reference is a qualitative sentence ("Qwen3.7-Max, is specifically engineered for agents and expands the frontier of model capabilities, including in core competencies such as agentic coding and complex reasoning") with zero benchmark figures anywhere in the document -- no MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, or any other named benchmark score appears in the filing at all. Also attempted the Q2 FY2026 earnings-call transcript on Seeking Alpha (blocked, HTTP 403) but a targeted web search of its content and other earnings-call summaries surfaced only qualitative "Qwen3.5-Plus... outstanding performance" language, no specific per-benchmark numbers for Qwen3.7-Max.

(4) GitHub issues / SDK repo discussions: Confirmed via the GitHub API that QwenLM's org repo list (github.com/orgs/QwenLM/repositories) has no dedicated "Qwen3.7" repo -- the newest dedicated model repo is "Qwen3.6". Their official CLI/SDK repo, QwenLM/qwen-code, does reference the model (PR #6978, "chore: update default model to qwen3.7-max"), confirming it's the right repo to check. Ran GitHub Search-API queries for issues/PRs combining "qwen3.7" with mmlu, humaneval, and aime across the entire QwenLM org: all three returned total_count: 0. A broader query for the literal string "MMLU" anywhere in QwenLM/qwen-code (title or body, no model-name filter) also returned total_count: 0 -- the term does not appear anywhere in that repo's issue/PR history. No maintainer-supplied benchmark number exists in this venue.

(5) Third-party review/analysis sites not yet checked this session: Targeted searches for interconnects.ai, Simon Willison (simonwillison.net), SemiAnalysis, and ChinaTalk coverage of Qwen3.7-Max specifically. Simon Willison's site has posts on Qwen3-Max, Qwen3-VL, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, and Qwen3.6-27B but no post on Qwen3.7-Max was found. SemiAnalysis search results surfaced only a chip-hardware analyst comment (Myron Xie) on Alibaba's T-Head accelerators, unrelated to LLM benchmarks. ChinaTalk returned no matching results at all. Directly fetched two independent analysis blogs that did cover Qwen3.7-Max in depth (officechai.com/ai/qwen-3-7-max-benchmarks and frankx.ai/blog/qwen3-max-analysis-2026) and read their full benchmark tables: both list only the already-corpus-known metrics (AA Intelligence Index 56.6, SWE-Bench Pro/Verified, Terminal-Bench 2.0, MCP-Atlas, GPQA Diamond, HMMT 2026 Feb, LiveCodeBench, MMLU-Pro, HLE, SciCode, Apex, CritPt, GDPval-AA, AA-Omniscience) -- neither reports plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval. Notably, frankx.ai's author explicitly states in-article: "I couldn't find published Qwen3.7-Max numbers for either from a source I trust" (referring to AIME 2026 and ARC-AGI-2), independently corroborating that a careful, source-conscious third-party analyst hit the same wall.

Net result after round 3: all four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) remain unresolved. Four of the five prescribed new source types (conference talks, investor/press materials, GitHub SDK-repo discussions, third-party analysis blogs) were fully checked and yielded clean, well-evidenced negatives. The fifth (Wayback Machine) could not be executed due to a hard access block/rate-limit on web.archive.org in this environment across seven distinct workaround attempts -- this remains a genuine open method for a future pass if archive.org access becomes available, rather than a confirmed negative. Given three consecutive independent research rounds (round 1: official/Epoch/Vals/LMArena/BenchLM/llm-stats; round 2: HELM/arXiv/GitHub-harness-logs/MathArena/LiveBench/OpenRouter/SEAL/regional-language/sibling-models; round 3: this pass) covering essentially every practicable open-source discovery channel with only the Wayback Machine left genuinely untested (due to environmental access failure, not a negative finding), this is recorded as omitted_no_evidence for all four benchmarks -- the weight of evidence strongly favors a genuine, durable absence (the Qwen3.7 generation appears to have retired plain MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval in favor of MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux/MMMLU, HMMT/Apex/IMOAnswerBench, and LiveCodeBench/SciCode/SWE-bench-Pro respectively, consistent with the generation-wide house style already documented in the round-1 notes). No additions made; the existing 14-entry results array is unchanged.

2026-07-18 (mmmu gap check, targeted single-benchmark pass): Confirmed architecturally inapplicable before any external search, per the corpus's own declared fields -- modalities: ["text"] and capabilities.vision: false for Qwen3.7-Max. This is not a fresh inference: the 2026-07-16 verification round already independently corroborated the same conclusion via Artificial Analysis's live Qwen3.7-Max model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/qwen3-7-max), which explicitly states the model "does not support image input. It can only process text." Alibaba's own launch blog (the primary source for this model's other 14 results) likewise contains no vision/multimodal benchmark rows for Qwen3.7-Max -- vision benchmarks (mmmu-pro, mathvision) in this generation are reported only by the sibling model Qwen3.7-Plus, which the corpus separately documents as vision-enabled. Given the model-level capability flag is authoritative and already cross-confirmed by an independent tracker, no further search (official materials, Epoch/Vals/LMArena, arXiv, GitHub harness logs, sibling tables) was performed for this gap, consistent with the task instruction to treat vision:false as settling the question rather than searching harder. Recorded as omitted_no_evidence -- architecturally inapplicable (text-only model), not an unpublished/missing score. No changes made to the existing 14-entry results array.
